examples.en: He is very meticulous when it comes to his work.
examples.ko: 그는 자기 일에 있어서 정말 꼼꼼해요.

examples.en: The project requires meticulous planning.
examples.ko: 그 프로젝트는 세심한 계획이 필요합니다.

examples.en: Let's cut to the chase. How much will this cost?
examples.ko: 본론으로 바로 들어가죠. 비용이 얼마나 들까요?

examples.en: I'll cut to the chase and tell you why I'm here.
examples.ko: 각설하고 제가 여기 온 이유를 말씀드릴게요.

examples.en: I'm feeling a bit under the weather today.

examples.en: She stayed home because she was under the weather.
examples.ko: 그녀는 몸이 안 좋아서 집에 있었어요.

examples.en: They had to call off the meeting due to the storm.
examples.ko: 폭풍 때문에 회의를 취소해야만 했어요.

examples.en: The wedding was called off at the last minute.
examples.ko: 결혼식이 마지막 순간에 취소되었습니다.

examples.en: He is a versatile actor who can play any role.
examples.ko: 그는 어떤 역할도 소화할 수 있는 다재다능한 배우예요.

examples.en: This tool is very versatile for home repairs.
examples.ko: 이 도구는 집 수리할 때 용도가 참 다양해요.

examples.en: She called me out of the blue after five years.

examples.en: The job offer came out of the blue.
examples.ko: 취업 제안이 갑자기 들어왔어요.

examples.en: This restaurant serves authentic Italian food.

examples.en: I love her authentic personality.
examples.ko: 그녀의 가식 없는 성격이 참 좋아요.

examples.en: I'm looking forward to meeting you next week.
examples.ko: 다음 주에 뵙기를 기대하고 있습니다.

examples.en: We are looking forward to our vacation.
examples.ko: 우리는 휴가를 손꼽아 기다리고 있어요.

examples.en: The exam was a piece of cake.
examples.ko: 시험은 식은 죽 먹기였어.

examples.en: His answer was a bit ambiguous.

examples.en: The instructions are too ambiguous to follow.

examples.en: Let's keep in touch after you move.

examples.en: It's hard to keep in touch with old friends.
examples.ko: 옛 친구들과 계속 연락하는 게 참 쉽지 않네요.

examples.en: I ran into my old teacher at the mall.
examples.ko: 쇼핑몰에서 옛날 선생님을 우연히 만났어.

examples.en: Guess who I ran into today!
examples.ko: 내가 오늘 누구 마주쳤는지 맞춰봐!

examples.en: There is a subtle difference between the two colors.
examples.ko: 두 색상 사이에는 미묘한 차이가 있어요.

examples.en: I noticed a subtle change in his attitude.
examples.ko: 그의 태도에서 미세한 변화를 느꼈어요.

examples.en: I'll buy you dinner to make up for being late.
examples.ko: 늦은 거 만회하게 내가 저녁 살게.

examples.en: Nothing can make up for the lost time.
examples.ko: 그 무엇도 잃어버린 시간을 되돌릴 수는 없어요.

examples.en: Children are often more resilient than adults.

examples.en: She's a resilient person who never gives up.

examples.en: We took a spontaneous trip to the beach.

examples.en: I love his spontaneous personality.
examples.ko: 나는 그의 즉흥적인 성격이 좋아.

examples.en: It’s hard at first, but you’ll get the hang of it soon.

examples.en: I'm finally getting the hang of this new software.
examples.ko: 드디어 이 새 소프트웨어 사용법에 익숙해지고 있어.

examples.en: We don't have a set plan, so let's just play it by ear.
examples.ko: 정해진 계획은 없으니까 그냥 상황 봐서 하자.

examples.en: I’m not sure when I’ll be free, let’s play it by ear.
examples.ko: 언제 한가해질지 모르겠으니 그때 가서 정하자.

examples.en: We should grab coffee and catch up with each other.
examples.ko: 우리 커피 마시면서 밀린 얘기 좀 하자.

examples.en: I need to catch up with my work after the vacation.
examples.ko: 휴가 끝나고 밀린 일을 좀 처리해야 해.

examples.en: I want to make sure we are all on the same page.
examples.ko: 우리 모두 같은 생각인지 확인하고 싶어요.

examples.en: Are we on the same page regarding the budget?

examples.en: Stop crying and pull yourself together.

examples.en: I took a deep breath to pull myself together before the interview.
examples.ko: 면접 전에 마음을 가다듬으려 깊게 숨을 들이마셨어.

2026-04-06_10.expression: on the tip of my tongue
2026-04-06_10.meaning_ko: 생각이 날 듯 말 듯 한

examples.en: His name is on the tip of my tongue, but I can't remember it.
examples.ko: 그 사람 이름이 생각날 듯 말 듯 한데 기억이 안 나네.

examples.en: The answer was on the tip of my tongue.
examples.ko: 답이 입안에서 맴돌았어.

examples.en: If you keep working like this, you'll burn out.
examples.ko: 너 이런 식으로 계속 일하면 완전히 지쳐버릴 거야.

examples.en: I'm feeling burnt out lately.
examples.ko: 나 요즘 번아웃 온 것 같아.

examples.en: Look on the bright side, at least you didn't lose your phone.
examples.ko: 긍정적으로 생각해, 적어도 핸드폰은 안 잃어버렸잖아.

examples.en: I always try to look on the bright side of things.
examples.ko: 난 항상 사물의 밝은 면을 보려고 노력해.

examples.en: I can't believe he stood me up last night.

examples.en: She's upset because her date stood her up.

examples.en: Could you keep an eye on my bag for a second?
examples.ko: 잠시 제 가방 좀 봐주실 수 있나요?

examples.en: The doctor told me to keep an eye on the symptoms.

examples.en: To make a long story short, we missed the flight.

examples.en: Anyway, to make a long story short, they got married.

examples.en: He was reluctant to share his personal information.

examples.en: I'm a bit reluctant to try that new restaurant.
examples.ko: 그 새로 생긴 식당에 가보는 게 좀 망설여져.

examples.en: Is this a genuine leather jacket?
examples.ko: 이거 진짜 가죽 재킷이에요?

examples.ko: 그녀는 내 건강을 진심으로 걱정해 주었다.

examples.en: Checking the data one by one was a tedious task.

examples.en: The long commute can be quite tedious.

examples.en: That sounds like a plausible explanation.
examples.ko: 그거 꽤 그럴듯한 설명인데.

examples.en: It's a plausible scenario, but we need more evidence.
examples.ko: 있을 법한 시나리오지만 증거가 더 필요해.

examples.en: Don't take your health for granted.

examples.en: Can you fill me in on what happened at the meeting?

examples.en: I'm glad we see eye to eye on this project.

examples.en: I love her vibrant personality; she always cheers me up.

synonyms.example: The party was very lively.
She is an energetic leader.

antonyms.example: The movie was a bit dull.

antonyms.example: We need more precise data.

examples.en: I'm a bit skeptical about this new diet plan.

examples.en: I'm a bit apprehensive about starting a new job tomorrow.

synonyms.example: She is anxious about the interview.
I felt uneasy about leaving her alone.

examples.en: You need to get a grip and focus on the presentation.
examples.ko: 정신 차리고 발표에 집중해야 해.

examples.en: I need to brush up on my English before my trip to London.

I need to polish my speech.

antonyms.example: Don't neglect your health.

Let's connect next week.

antonyms.example: We lost touch after graduation.

examples.en: Does the name 'James Kim' ring a bell?

examples.en: The title of the movie rings a bell, but I don't remember the plot.

examples.en: He pulled a fast one and sold me a broken phone.

examples.en: Even though he's a CEO, he's very down to earth.

examples.en: I don't know why she's giving me the cold shoulder.

The noise was constant all night.

antonyms.example: Her performance has been inconsistent lately.

examples.en: The second paragraph is redundant and can be removed.
examples.ko: 두 번째 문단은 내용이 중복되니까 삭제해도 돼.

examples.en: New technology has made manual labor redundant in this factory.
examples.ko: 새로운 기술 때문에 이 공장에서 수작업은 더 이상 필요 없게 됐어.

synonyms.example: Avoid adding unnecessary details.
That explanation is superfluous.

antonyms.example: Water is essential for life.

examples.en: He had to pull strings to get his son into that school.
examples.ko: 그는 아들을 그 학교에 입학시키려고 인맥을 동원해야 했다.

examples.en: Can you pull some strings and get us a table tonight?
examples.ko: 어떻게 손 좀 써서 오늘 밤 우리 자리 좀 잡아줄 수 있어?

synonyms.example: He used his influence to help me.

examples.en: I don't speak French, but I got the gist of what they said.
examples.ko: 프랑스어는 못하지만 그들이 무슨 말을 하는지 대충은 알아들었어.

examples.en: Just give me the gist of the meeting; I don't need all the details.
examples.ko: 회의 요점만 말해줘. 세세한 건 필요 없어.

synonyms.example: I think I get the drift of your idea.

examples.en: I can't put up with this noise anymore.
examples.ko: 이 소음 더 이상은 못 참겠어.

examples.en: How do you put up with his constant complaining?
examples.ko: 걔가 맨날 불평하는 걸 어떻게 다 받아주고 있어?

synonyms.example: I won't tolerate such behavior.
They had to endure a long winter.

examples.en: You can always count on me if you need help.
examples.ko: 도움이 필요하면 언제든 나 믿어도 돼.

examples.en: I was counting on the rain to stop before the picnic.
examples.ko: 소풍 가기 전에 비가 그칠 거라고 믿고 있었는데.

synonyms.example: You can rely on my support.
Don't bank on a pay rise yet.

examples.en: It's a long shot, but I'm going to apply for the job anyway.
examples.ko: 가망은 별로 없지만, 그래도 그 일자리에 지원해 보려고.

examples.en: I know it's a long shot, but do you happen to have a spare ticket?
examples.ko: 안 될 가능성이 높다는 건 알지만, 혹시 남는 티켓 있어?

synonyms.example: There is a slim chance of recovery.

antonyms.example: Winning this contract is a sure thing.

examples.en: Do you have a charger I could borrow, by any chance?
examples.ko: 혹시 제가 빌릴 수 있는 충전기 있을까요?

examples.en: Are you, by any chance, free this afternoon?
examples.ko: 혹시 오늘 오후에 시간 좀 되니?

synonyms.example: Are you perchance looking for this?

examples.en: I'm keeping my fingers crossed for your interview results.
examples.ko: 네 면접 결과 잘 나오길 빌고 있을게.

examples.en: We're keeping our fingers crossed that the weather stays clear.
examples.ko: 날씨가 계속 맑기를 간절히 바라고 있어.

synonyms.example: We just have to hope for the best.

examples.en: Stop beating around the bush and tell me what you really want.
examples.ko: 말 돌리지 말고 진짜 원하는 게 뭔지 말해봐.

examples.en: He beat around the bush for ten minutes before asking for a loan.
examples.ko: 그는 돈을 빌려달라고 하기 전까지 10분 동안 딴소리만 늘어놨다.

synonyms.example: Stop speaking indirectly and tell the truth.

antonyms.example: Let's get straight to the point.

examples.en: The party got a bit out of hand when too many people showed up.
examples.ko: 사람들이 너무 많이 오는 바람에 파티가 좀 감당 안 되게 흘러갔어.

examples.en: They rejected my proposal out of hand without even reading it.
examples.ko: 그들은 내 제안서를 읽어보지도 않고 단칼에 거절했다.

synonyms.example: The fire quickly got out of control.

antonyms.example: Everything is under control now.

examples.en: I don't have a specific plan for the trip; I'll just go with the flow.
examples.ko: 이번 여행에 딱히 계획은 없어. 그냥 상황 봐서 하려고.

examples.en: She's very easygoing and usually just goes with the flow.
examples.ko: 그녀는 성격이 좋아서 보통 대세를 따르는 편이야.

synonyms.example: Sometimes it's easier to follow the crowd.
You need to adapt to new environments.

examples.en: I used to be a great cook, but I think I'm losing my touch.
examples.ko: 나 예전엔 요리 진짜 잘했는데, 이제 감을 잃어가는 것 같아.

examples.en: Don't worry, you haven't lost your touch; that was a great shot!
examples.ko: 걱정 마, 실력 안 죽었네. 방금 샷 정말 좋았어!

synonyms.example: His health began to decline.

antonyms.en: be at the top of one's game
antonyms.example: She is currently at the top of her game.

examples.en: I'm feeling a bit overwhelmed with all the new responsibilities.
examples.ko: 새로운 책임들 때문에 좀 벅차다고 느껴져.

examples.en: The amount of information in the report was overwhelming.
examples.ko: 보고서의 정보량이 너무 방대해서 압도적이었어.

synonyms.example: I'm swamped with emails.
She's stressed out about exams.

antonyms.example: He remained calm under pressure.

examples.en: She's a very diligent student, always submitting her assignments on time.
examples.ko: 그녀는 정말 성실한 학생이라 항상 숙제를 제시간에 제출해.

examples.en: His diligent efforts paid off, and he got promoted.
examples.ko: 그의 꾸준한 노력이 결실을 맺어 승진했어.

synonyms.example: She is an assiduous student.
He's an industrious worker.

antonyms.example: Don't be lazy; finish your tasks.

examples.en: You need to be more assertive if you want to get your ideas heard.
examples.ko: 네 생각을 인정받으려면 좀 더 적극적으로 주장해야 해.

examples.en: She learned to be assertive without being rude.
examples.ko: 그녀는 무례하지 않으면서도 자기주장을 하는 법을 배웠다.

synonyms.example: He spoke with a confident voice.
She took a firm stance.

antonyms.example: Don't be passive; speak up.
He was too submissive to argue.

examples.en: The company is known for its innovative products and services.
examples.ko: 그 회사는 혁신적인 제품과 서비스로 유명하다.

examples.en: We need to find innovative solutions to these problems.
examples.ko: 우리는 이 문제들에 대한 혁신적인 해결책을 찾아야 해.

synonyms.example: He has a very creative mind.
That's an original idea.

antonyms.example: They stick to traditional methods.
It was a conventional approach.

examples.en: A good leader needs to be empathetic to their team members.
examples.ko: 좋은 리더는 팀원들에게 공감할 줄 알아야 한다.

examples.en: She has an empathetic approach to her patients' concerns.
examples.ko: 그녀는 환자들의 걱정에 대해 공감하는 태도를 보인다.

synonyms.example: She's a very understanding friend.
He showed compassionate care.

antonyms.example: He was indifferent to their suffering.
The public became apathetic.

examples.en: I can't make it to dinner tonight, but can I take a rain check?
examples.ko: 오늘 저녁 식사는 못 갈 것 같은데, 다음으로 미룰 수 있을까?

examples.en: Thanks for the invitation, but I'll have to take a rain check this time.
examples.ko: 초대해줘서 고맙지만, 이번에는 다음 기회로 미뤄야겠어.

synonyms.example: Let's postpone the meeting.
Can we reschedule for next week?

antonyms.example: I accept your invitation.
Don't hesitate, just go for it.

examples.en: The project is tough, but we just have to bite the bullet and finish it.
examples.ko: 프로젝트가 힘들지만, 우리는 이를 악물고 끝내야 해.

examples.en: I didn't want to work overtime, but I had to bite the bullet.
examples.ko: 야근하기 싫었지만, 어쩔 수 없이 참아야 했다.

synonyms.example: I had to grin and bear it.
You just have to tough it out.

antonyms.example: Don't give up so easily.
He tried to avoid the problem.

examples.en: It's getting late, we should probably hit the road.
examples.ko: 늦었으니 이제 출발해야 할 것 같아.

examples.en: Let's hit the road before traffic gets bad.
examples.ko: 차가 막히기 전에 출발하자.

synonyms.example: We set off early in the morning.
The train departs at noon.

antonyms.example: We arrived at the destination.
I decided to stay put.

examples.en: Don't spill the beans about the surprise party!
examples.ko: 깜짝 파티에 대해 비밀을 누설하지 마!

examples.en: I accidentally spilled the beans about his promotion.
examples.ko: 내가 실수로 그의 승진 소식을 말해버렸어.

synonyms.en: let the cat out of the bag
synonyms.example: She let the cat out of the bag.
He revealed a secret.

antonyms.example: Can you keep a secret?
He kept silent about it.

examples.en: Our team always goes the extra mile to satisfy our clients.
examples.ko: 우리 팀은 항상 고객을 만족시키기 위해 특별히 더 노력한다.

examples.en: She's a great employee because she always goes the extra mile.
examples.ko: 그녀는 항상 더 노력하기 때문에 훌륭한 직원이다.

synonyms.example: You need to make an effort.
He exerted himself to finish.

antonyms.example: Don't just do the bare minimum.
He tends to slack off.

examples.en: He was going to propose, but he got cold feet at the last minute.
examples.ko: 그는 프러포즈할 생각이었는데, 마지막 순간에 겁을 먹었어.

examples.en: Don't get cold feet now; we've come too far to give up.
examples.ko: 이제 와서 겁먹지 마. 너무 멀리 왔으니 포기할 수 없어.

synonyms.example: I'm having second thoughts.
He backed out of the deal.

antonyms.example: She was determined to win.
You should be confident.

examples.en: He tends to lose his temper when he's stressed.
examples.ko: 그는 스트레스를 받으면 욱하는 경향이 있어.

examples.en: I'm sorry I lost my temper earlier; I didn't mean it.
examples.ko: 아까 화낸 거 미안해. 일부러 그런 건 아니었어.

synonyms.example: He got angry at me.
She blew a fuse when she heard.

antonyms.example: Try to keep calm.
He managed to stay cool.

examples.en: She studied hard and passed her final exams with flying colors.
examples.ko: 그녀는 열심히 공부해서 기말고사를 아주 훌륭하게 통과했다.

examples.en: The team presented their project and passed with flying colors.
examples.ko: 팀은 프로젝트를 발표했고, 아주 훌륭하게 통과했다.

synonyms.example: She excels in math.
They succeeded brilliantly.

antonyms.example: He failed miserably.
I barely passed the exam.

examples.en: I know you're struggling with the workload; we're all in the same boat.
examples.ko: 업무량 때문에 힘든 거 알아. 우리 모두 같은 처지야.

examples.en: We're both new to this city, so we're kind of in the same boat.
examples.ko: 우리 둘 다 이 도시에 처음 왔으니, 좀 같은 처지라고 할 수 있지.

synonyms.example: We share a common situation.
They face the same challenge.

antonyms.example: I'm in a different situation.
He had an advantage.

examples.en: You have to read between the lines to understand what he really means.
examples.ko: 그가 진짜 무슨 의미인지 이해하려면 속뜻을 파악해야 해.

examples.en: After their conversation, I could read between the lines that they weren't happy.
examples.ko: 그들의 대화 후에, 나는 그들이 행복하지 않다는 속뜻을 읽을 수 있었다.

synonyms.example: I understood the implication.
She grasped the hidden meaning.

antonyms.example: Don't take it literally.
You missed the point.

examples.en: The city is notorious for its terrible traffic jams.
examples.ko: 그 도시는 끔찍한 교통 체증으로 악명이 높아요.

examples.en: He's notorious for being late to every meeting.
examples.ko: 그는 모든 회의에 늦기로 유명해.

synonyms.example: He is infamous for his short temper.
This is an ill-famed neighborhood.

antonyms.example: We only deal with reputable companies.

examples.en: I didn't mean to imply that it was your fault.
examples.ko: 네 잘못이라고 말하려던 의도는 아니었어.

examples.en: Silence doesn't always imply agreement.
examples.ko: 침묵이 항상 동의를 의미하는 것은 아니다.

synonyms.example: Are you suggesting that I'm lying?
She hinted that she might resign.

antonyms.example: Please state your name clearly.

examples.en: The room was small but adequate for my needs.
examples.ko: 방은 작았지만 내가 쓰기엔 충분했다.

examples.en: Make sure you have adequate lighting in your workspace.
examples.ko: 작업 공간에 조명이 충분한지 확인하세요.

synonyms.example: We have sufficient funds for the trip.
The hotel was decent for the price.

antonyms.example: The evidence was insufficient to prove him guilty.

examples.en: If the pain is persistent, you should see a doctor.
examples.ko: 통증이 계속되면 의사를 만나보셔야 해요.

examples.en: She was persistent in her efforts to get the job.
examples.ko: 그녀는 그 직업을 얻기 위해 끈질기게 노력했다.

synonyms.example: He is a tenacious negotiator.
I'm tired of your constant complaints.

antonyms.example: We experienced intermittent rain showers.

examples.en: Let's wrap up this meeting and go to lunch.
examples.ko: 이 회의는 이만 마무리하고 점심 먹으러 갑시다.

examples.en: I need about ten more minutes to wrap things up here.
examples.ko: 여기 일 좀 마무리하는 데 10분만 더 있으면 돼.

synonyms.example: Let's finish up the report.
The meeting concluded at noon.

antonyms.example: We'll kick off the project tomorrow.

examples.en: Who wants to get the ball rolling on this discussion?
examples.ko: 누가 이 토론을 먼저 시작해 보실 분 있나요?

examples.en: We need to get the ball rolling if we want to finish by Friday.
examples.ko: 금요일까지 끝내려면 이제 슬슬 시작해야 해.

synonyms.example: Let's get started on the work.
They initiated a new marketing plan.

antonyms.example: The negotiations have stalled.

examples.en: I'm just trying to keep a low profile until things settle down.
examples.ko: 상황이 진정될 때까지 그냥 조용히 있으려고 해.

examples.en: The celebrity tried to keep a low profile while on vacation.
examples.ko: 그 연예인은 휴가 동안 눈에 띄지 않으려고 애썼다.

synonyms.example: I'm going to lay low for a while.
He prefers to stay out of the spotlight.

antonyms.example: She really stands out in a crowd.

examples.en: I made it to the gate just as they were closing, which was cutting it close.
examples.ko: 게이트가 닫히기 직전에 도착했는데, 정말 아슬아슬했어.

examples.en: We're cutting it close with the budget this month.
examples.ko: 이번 달 예산이 정말 아슬아슬하네.

synonyms.example: Time is running tight for us.

examples.en: The client rejected our proposal, so it's back to square one.
examples.ko: 클라이언트가 제안을 거절해서 처음부터 다시 해야 해.

examples.en: If this experiment fails, we'll be back to square one.
examples.ko: 이 실험이 실패하면 우린 다시 원점으로 돌아가는 거야.

synonyms.example: We have to start over from scratch.
The plan failed, so it's back to the drawing board.

examples.en: We're looking into the possibility of opening a new branch.
examples.ko: 새 지점을 낼 가능성을 검토 중입니다.

examples.en: I'll look into why your order hasn't arrived yet.
examples.ko: 주문하신 상품이 왜 아직 안 왔는지 확인해 보겠습니다.

synonyms.example: The police are investigating the case.
I'll check out that new restaurant.

examples.en: He was hired on the spot after the interview.
examples.ko: 그는 면접 직후 그 자리에서 바로 채용되었다.

examples.en: I hate to put you on the spot, but do you have the answer?
examples.ko: 곤란하게 만들고 싶진 않지만, 답을 알고 있나요?

synonyms.example: He answered the question immediately.
Do it right away.

examples.en: Please keep in mind that the office will be closed tomorrow.
examples.ko: 내일은 사무실이 문을 닫는다는 점을 유념해 주세요.

examples.en: Keep in mind that prices may vary depending on the season.
examples.ko: 계절에 따라 가격이 달라질 수 있다는 걸 기억하세요.

synonyms.example: Bear in mind that it's a long journey.
Remember to lock the door.

antonyms.example: Don't forget your umbrella.

examples.en: It turned out to be a beautiful day after all.
examples.ko: 결국 날씨가 아주 화창해졌어.

examples.en: As it turns out, I didn't need to worry so much.
examples.ko: 나중에 알고 보니 그렇게 걱정할 필요가 없었더라고.

synonyms.example: We ended up staying home.
The rumor proved to be true.

examples.en: Working late every night is taking a toll on my health.
examples.ko: 매일 밤 늦게까지 일하는 게 내 건강에 타격을 주고 있어.

examples.en: The bad economy has taken a toll on small businesses.
examples.ko: 불황이 소상공인들에게 큰 타격을 입혔다.

synonyms.example: Smoking can damage your lungs.
The news affected him deeply.

examples.en: Don't put off until tomorrow what you can do today.
examples.ko: 오늘 할 수 있는 일을 내일로 미루지 마라.

examples.en: We had to put off the wedding until next year.
examples.ko: 우리는 결혼식을 내년으로 미뤄야만 했다.

synonyms.example: The match was postponed due to rain.
Flight departures were delayed.

antonyms.example: We need to expedite the shipping process.

examples.en: The new operating system is very intuitive.
examples.ko: 새 운영체제는 정말 직관적이라 쓰기 편해.

examples.en: I need an intuitive design for my website.
examples.ko: 내 웹사이트에는 딱 보면 알 수 있는 디자인이 필요해.

synonyms.example: The interface is very user-friendly.
His reaction was purely instinctive.

antonyms.example: The result seems counterintuitive at first.

examples.en: We had to compromise on the color of the car.
examples.ko: 우리는 자동차 색상에 대해 타협을 해야 했다.

examples.en: It's important to compromise in a relationship.
examples.ko: 인간관계에서는 서로 양보하는 것이 중요하다.

synonyms.example: Let's meet halfway on the price.

antonyms.example: They are still in a dispute over the land.

examples.en: Let's take a pragmatic approach to this problem.
examples.ko: 이 문제에 대해 좀 더 현실적으로 접근해 봅시다.

examples.en: He made a pragmatic decision to save the company.
examples.ko: 그는 회사를 살리기 위해 실용적인 결정을 내렸다.

synonyms.example: She has a very practical approach to life.
We need a realistic plan for this project.

antonyms.example: He is too idealistic for this job.

examples.en: I want your candid opinion on my new book.
examples.ko: 내 새 책에 대한 네 솔직한 의견을 듣고 싶어.

examples.en: The interview was surprisingly candid.
examples.ko: 그 인터뷰는 놀라울 정도로 솔직했다.

synonyms.example: To be frank, I don't like the idea.
She gave me a straightforward answer.

antonyms.example: I felt his apology was insincere.

examples.en: Why did you bring that up now?
examples.ko: 왜 그 이야기를 지금 꺼내는 거야?

examples.en: I didn't want to bring up the budget issue during dinner.
examples.ko: 저녁 식사 자리에서 예산 문제를 꺼내고 싶지 않았어.

synonyms.example: Did he mention the meeting?
I'd like to raise an important point.

antonyms.example: She chose to ignore my comment.

examples.en: It's hard to keep track of all these passwords.
examples.ko: 이 비밀번호들을 다 기억하고 관리하기가 힘드네.

examples.en: I use an app to keep track of my daily steps.
examples.ko: 나는 매일 걸음 수를 기록하기 위해 앱을 써.

synonyms.example: We need to monitor our progress.
I stay updated on the news.

antonyms.example: I lost track of time while reading.

examples.en: It seems expensive, but it'll save money in the long run.
examples.ko: 비싸 보이지만, 장기적으로 보면 돈을 아껴줄 거야.

examples.en: Exercising every day pays off in the long run.
examples.ko: 매일 운동하는 것은 결국 다 도움이 돼.

synonyms.example: He eventually found his keys.
Ultimately, the choice is yours.

antonyms.example: It's a good plan in the short term.

examples.en: We can't rule out the possibility of a mistake.
examples.ko: 실수했을 가능성을 배제할 순 없어.

examples.en: The police have ruled out him as a suspect.
examples.ko: 경찰은 그를 용의자 선상에서 제외했다.

synonyms.example: The cost of lunch is excluded.
We eliminated the wrong options.

antonyms.example: Please include me in the email.

examples.en: I wanted to get a head start on my holiday shopping.
examples.ko: 연휴 쇼핑을 남들보다 좀 일찍 시작하고 싶었어.

examples.en: Studying early will give you a head start in class.
examples.ko: 미리 공부하면 수업 시간에 유리할 거야.

synonyms.example: Let's get an early start tomorrow.

antonyms.example: I don't want to fall behind schedule.

examples.en: Every small donation makes a difference.
examples.ko: 작은 기부 하나하나가 큰 변화를 만듭니다.

examples.en: Does it really make a difference which one I choose?
examples.ko: 내가 어떤 걸 고르든 정말 큰 상관이 있어?

synonyms.example: Your opinion matters to us.
This will impact our future.

examples.en: Take it easy! I didn't mean to offend you.
examples.ko: 진정해! 기분 나쁘게 하려던 건 아니었어.

examples.en: I'm just going to take it easy this weekend.
examples.ko: 이번 주말엔 그냥 무리 안 하고 푹 쉴 거야.

synonyms.example: I just want to relax this weekend.
Please calm down and tell me what happened.

antonyms.example: You need to work hard to succeed.

examples.en: He's smart, and on top of that, he's very kind.
examples.ko: 그는 똑똑한데다 친절하기까지 해.

examples.en: My car broke down, and on top of that, I lost my wallet.
examples.ko: 차가 고장 났는데, 설상가상으로 지갑까지 잃어버렸어.

synonyms.example: Besides, it's too late now.
Moreover, the price is reasonable.

examples.en: We're running out of milk. Can you buy some?
examples.ko: 우유가 다 떨어져 가. 좀 사다 줄래?

examples.en: I'm running out of patience with this slow computer.
examples.ko: 이 느린 컴퓨터 때문에 인내심이 바닥나고 있어.

synonyms.example: We used up all the paper.

antonyms.example: We have plenty of time.

examples.en: I've always looked up to my older brother.
examples.ko: 나는 항상 우리 형을 존경해 왔어.

examples.en: Many young players look up to him as a legend.
examples.ko: 많은 어린 선수들이 그를 전설로 우러러본다.

synonyms.example: I really admire your courage.
You should respect your elders.

antonyms.example: Never look down on anyone.

examples.en: In a nutshell, the plan was a complete failure.
examples.ko: 간단히 말해서, 그 계획은 완전히 실패였어.

examples.en: Can you tell me what happened in a nutshell?
examples.ko: 무슨 일이 있었는지 짧게 요약해서 말해줄래?

synonyms.example: To sum up, we need more funds.
Basically, it's a great deal.

examples.en: Avoid any strenuous exercise for a few days.
examples.ko: 며칠 동안은 격렬한 운동은 피하세요.

examples.en: It was a strenuous day at the office.
examples.ko: 사무실에서 정말 기 빨리는 하루였어.

synonyms.example: It was an arduous climb to the top.
The job is very demanding physically.

antonyms.example: She made the dance look effortless.

examples.en: He was oblivious to the fact that everyone was staring.
examples.ko: 그는 모든 사람이 쳐다보고 있다는 사실을 전혀 모르고 있었다.

examples.en: She seemed oblivious to the noise outside.
examples.ko: 그녀는 밖에서 나는 소음에는 전혀 신경을 안 쓰는 듯했다.

synonyms.example: He was unaware of the danger.
She was unconscious of her own habits.

antonyms.example: Be mindful of your surroundings.

examples.en: Starting a new job can be a daunting task.
examples.ko: 새 직장을 시작하는 건 정말 벅찬 일일 수 있어.

examples.en: The thought of moving abroad was a bit daunting.
examples.ko: 해외로 이사한다는 생각에 좀 막막했어.

synonyms.example: The interview was intimidating.

antonyms.example: The feedback was encouraging.

examples.en: They are quite indifferent to their neighbors' problems.
examples.ko: 그들은 이웃의 문제에 상당히 무관심하다.

examples.en: I'm indifferent to which movie we watch.
examples.ko: 어떤 영화를 보든 난 상관없어(시큰둥해).

synonyms.example: She seemed uncaring about the result.
He remained aloof from the group.

antonyms.example: He is passionate about music.

examples.en: I really get a kick out of watching these old cartoons.
examples.ko: 난 이 옛날 만화들을 보는 게 너무 재밌어.

examples.en: He gets a kick out of making people laugh.
examples.ko: 그는 사람들을 웃기는 데서 큰 재미를 느껴.

synonyms.example: I enjoy playing the piano.
She gets a thrill out of skydiving.

antonyms.example: I'm bored by this movie.

examples.en: What kind of skills can you bring to the table?
examples.ko: 당신은 우리 팀에 어떤 역량을 기여할 수 있나요?

examples.en: He brings years of experience to the table.
examples.ko: 그는 수년간의 풍부한 경험을 팀에 제공합니다.

synonyms.example: Everyone should contribute ideas.
She has a lot to offer the company.

antonyms.example: That comment took away from her point.

2026-04-09_73898_07.expression: take something with a grain of salt

examples.en: You should take his advice with a grain of salt.
examples.ko: 그의 조언은 좀 걸러서 듣는 게 좋아.

examples.en: I took the news with a grain of salt until I saw the proof.
examples.ko: 나는 증거를 보기 전까지는 그 소식을 곧이곧대로 믿지 않았다.

synonyms.example: I'm skeptical about his story.

antonyms.example: Don't take everything at face value.

2026-04-09_73898_08.expression: off the top of my head

examples.en: I can't remember the name off the top of my head.
examples.ko: 지금 당장은 이름이 바로 안 떠오르네.

examples.en: Off the top of my head, I think there are about ten people coming.
examples.ko: 얼핏 생각하기로는 열 명 정도 오는 것 같아.

synonyms.example: I can't tell you offhand.

antonyms.example: After careful thought, I decided to quit.

examples.en: We need to take the weather into account for the picnic.
examples.ko: 소풍 가려면 날씨를 고려해야 해.

examples.en: He didn't take into account the time difference.
examples.ko: 그는 시차를 참작하지 않았다.

synonyms.example: We must consider all options.
Did you factor in the shipping costs?

antonyms.example: You shouldn't ignore the risks.

examples.en: The view from the top just blew me away.
examples.ko: 정상에서 본 풍경은 정말 입이 떡 벌어질 정도였어.

examples.en: Her voice will blow you away.
examples.ko: 그녀의 목소리를 들으면 정말 깜짝 놀랄 거야.

synonyms.example: His performance impressed me.
The magic trick amazed the kids.

antonyms.example: The ending disappointed me.

examples.en: She really stands out in a crowd.
examples.ko: 그녀는 군중 속에서도 정말 눈에 띄어.

examples.en: His resume stood out from the rest.
examples.ko: 그의 이력서는 다른 것들보다 훨씬 돋보였다.

synonyms.example: The stain is very noticeable.

antonyms.example: He tried to blend in with the crowd.

examples.en: I'm going to play it safe and leave early.
examples.ko: 안전하게 그냥 좀 일찍 출발할게.

examples.en: Instead of investing, he decided to play it safe.
examples.ko: 그는 투자하는 대신 신중하게 행동하기로 했다.

synonyms.example: I'll take no chances and bring an umbrella.

antonyms.example: Sometimes you have to take a risk.

examples.en: I was going to ask her out, but I lost my nerve.
examples.ko: 데이트 신청을 하려고 했는데, 막판에 겁이 나서 못 했어.

examples.en: Don't lose your nerve now; you're almost there!
examples.ko: 지금 와서 겁먹지 마. 거의 다 왔어!

synonyms.example: He chickened out at the last minute.

antonyms.example: She kept her cool during the fire.

examples.en: Our travel plans fell through because of the strike.
examples.ko: 파업 때문에 우리 여행 계획이 무산됐어.

examples.en: The house sale fell through at the last minute.
examples.ko: 주택 매매가 마지막 순간에 엎어졌다.

synonyms.example: The deal failed to close.

antonyms.example: The plan succeeded eventually.

examples.en: Could you look over my essay before I hand it in?
examples.ko: 제출하기 전에 내 에세이 좀 봐줄 수 있어?

examples.en: I need some time to look over the contract.
examples.ko: 계약서를 훑어볼 시간이 좀 필요해요.

synonyms.example: Please review the document.
He scanned the list for his name.

antonyms.example: I overlooked a small error.

examples.en: Sorry, I didn't hear you. I was a bit preoccupied with work.
examples.ko: 미안, 못 들었어. 일 때문에 정신이 좀 팔려 있어서.

examples.en: He seemed preoccupied during the entire meeting.
examples.ko: 그는 회의 내내 딴생각을 하는 것 같았다.

synonyms.example: He was too distracted to drive safely.
She's been a bit absent-minded lately.

antonyms.example: The staff was very attentive to our needs.

examples.en: I felt a bit intimidated by all the experts in the room.
examples.ko: 방 안에 있는 전문가들 때문에 좀 위축됐어.

examples.en: Don't let his bossy attitude intimidate you.
examples.ko: 그의 고압적인 태도에 기죽지 마.

synonyms.example: Don't be daunted by the size of the task.
She was fearful of making a mistake.

antonyms.example: You need to be confident during the interview.

examples.en: I get easily flustered when I have to speak in public.
examples.ko: 나는 사람들 앞에서 말해야 할 때 쉽게 당황해서 허둥대.

examples.en: She looked flustered when she realized she was late.
examples.ko: 그녀는 늦었다는 걸 깨닫고 허둥지둥하는 모습이었다.

synonyms.example: He got rattled by the difficult questions.
She became increasingly agitated as the wait grew longer.

antonyms.example: She remained composed despite the crisis.

examples.en: I'm not exaggerating, it was the best meal I've ever had.
examples.ko: 과장하는 게 아니라, 진짜 내 인생 최고의 식사였어.

examples.en: Stop exaggerating! It wasn't that cold outside.
examples.ko: 오버 좀 하지 마! 밖이 그렇게 춥진 않았어.

synonyms.example: It's easy to overstate the importance of this news.

antonyms.example: He tends to understate his achievements.

examples.en: We need to get to the bottom of this technical issue.
examples.ko: 우리는 이 기술적인 문제의 원인을 확실히 밝혀내야 해.

examples.en: I'm determined to get to the bottom of why she's so upset.
examples.ko: 그녀가 왜 그렇게 화가 났는지 꼭 알아내고 말 거야.

synonyms.example: The police are investigating the cause of the fire.
We need to dig deeper to find the truth.

antonyms.example: This report only scratches the surface of the problem.

examples.en: I'm sorry to put you on the spot, but do you have the answer?
examples.ko: 곤란하게 해서 미안한데, 혹시 답을 알고 있니?

examples.en: The reporter really put the politician on the spot with that question.
examples.ko: 그 기자는 질문 하나로 정치인을 아주 난처하게 만들었다.

synonyms.example: I didn't mean to embarrass you in front of everyone.

examples.en: His sudden proposal caught her completely off guard.
examples.ko: 그의 갑작스러운 프로포즈에 그녀는 완전히 허를 찔렸다.

examples.en: The difficult question caught the speaker off guard.
examples.ko: 그 어려운 질문은 강연자를 당황하게 만들었다.

synonyms.example: The news surprised everyone.
The sudden rain took us by surprise.

examples.en: I had to burn the midnight oil to prepare for the presentation.
examples.ko: 발표 준비를 하느라 밤늦게까지 일해야 했어.

examples.en: Students often burn the midnight oil during finals week.
examples.ko: 학생들은 기말고사 기간에 종종 밤을 새우며 공부한다.

synonyms.example: I had to stay up late to finish the report.
He's been working overtime every day this week.

antonyms.example: I'm exhausted, let's call it a night.

2026-04-10_60003_09.expression: have a lot on one's plate

examples.en: I'd love to help, but I have a lot on my plate right now.
examples.ko: 돕고 싶지만, 지금 당장 할 일이 너무 많아.

examples.en: With the new project and the baby, she has a lot on her plate.
examples.ko: 새 프로젝트에 아기까지 있어서 그녀는 정신없이 바쁘다.

synonyms.example: I'm snowed under with paperwork right now.
I'm tied up in meetings all afternoon.

antonyms.example: Now that I've retired, I have a lot of time on my hands.

examples.en: In this house, my wife is the one who calls the shots.
examples.ko: 우리 집에서는 우리 아내가 모든 결정을 다 해.

examples.en: You're the boss, so you get to call the shots.
examples.ko: 당신이 상사니까, 당신이 결정해야죠.

synonyms.example: Who is in charge of this department?
He's the one pulling the strings behind the scenes.

antonyms.example: I'm just here to follow orders.

examples.en: I promised to be there, and I don't want to let her down.
examples.ko: 거기 가기로 약속했어, 그녀를 실망시키고 싶지 않아.

examples.en: Don't worry, I won't let you down this time.
examples.ko: 걱정 마, 이번에는 절대 실망시키지 않을게.

synonyms.example: I'm sorry to disappoint you.

antonyms.example: I want to make my parents proud.

examples.en: If we want to win, we really need to step up our game.
examples.ko: 우리가 이기려면 정말 더 분발해야 해.

examples.en: He stepped up his game and got promoted within a year.
examples.ko: 그는 실력을 끌어올려서 1년 만에 승진했다.

synonyms.example: You need to improve your English skills.
He pushed himself to the limit.

antonyms.example: Don't slacken off just because the deadline is far away.

examples.en: Our new manager runs a tight ship, so don't be late.
examples.ko: 우리 새 매니저님은 아주 엄격하시니까 늦지 마.

examples.en: She runs a tight ship at home; everything is always organized.
examples.ko: 그녀는 집안일을 아주 빈틈없이 해내서 모든 게 항상 정리되어 있다.

synonyms.example: The manager keeps a tight rein on spending.

antonyms.example: The security at the gate was a bit lax.

examples.en: Please keep me in the loop regarding the meeting schedule.
examples.ko: 회의 일정 관련해서 계속 저한테도 알려주세요.

examples.en: Thanks for keeping me in the loop; I appreciate the update.
examples.ko: 계속 상황 공유해 줘서 고마워. 업데이트해 줘서 정말 다행이야.

synonyms.example: Please keep me informed of any changes.
I'll keep you updated on the progress.

antonyms.example: They left me in the dark about the layoffs.

examples.en: Ten years of hard work just went down the drain.
examples.ko: 10년 동안 공들인 노력이 그냥 수포로 돌아갔어.

examples.en: I don't want all this money to go down the drain.
examples.ko: 이 모든 돈이 그냥 허무하게 날아가는 건 원치 않아.

synonyms.example: All that food went to waste.
All our efforts were in vain.

antonyms.example: Hard work always pays off in the end.
